hints.text: Plates long white with **blue strip left** (like most of Europe)
**Turkish**: Ç Ş (cedilla), Ö Ü, Ğ (breve), capital **İ with dot** — and the **dotless ı**, essentially unique to Turkish
hints.uniq_note: The dotless ı is essentially unique to Turkish — Azerbaijani uses it too but has no Street View; within covered countries it means Turkey.

hints.text: Bollards: rectangular **plain white + red front reflector**, thin from the side (rare yellow snow version)

shared_with.note: Romania has similar bollards — but they are rare there.
shared_with.diff: Romanian language (ă î ș ț) → Romania; dotless ı → Turkey.

hints.text: Road lines inconsistent: white, yellow, or mixed; **salt-and-pepper pavement** common; **divided highways extremely common** for a Mediterranean country
Chevrons: red-on-white (tip doesn't touch the edge, sometimes neon-yellow border); intersections: **black-yellow multi-arrow chevrons pointing up**; narrow red-white variant (≈ South Africa's)
Direction signs: distinct font with **noticeably wide letter spacing**; snow poles black-orange-black-white; stop sign says **DUR**
**Both A-type AND B-type guardrails** — seeing both excludes all of southern Europe/Middle East except Montenegro and Israel & the West Bank
Metal poles: **ladder-shaped or mesh pattern** — extremely common
Gen3: **never an antenna**; Gen4: white car (sliver of the back, short/no antenna) + **grey pickup truck** in some areas + **smallcam** (low mount, big blur w/ front protrusion)

shared_with.note: Similar grey pickup exists in Kenya (also Georgia, Senegal, Rwanda, Ghana, Paraguay).
shared_with.diff: African savanna vs Anatolian steppe; Turkish signage decides.

shared_with.note: Similar pickup (also in Georgia and Paraguay — no separate pages here).

hints.text: Landscape: arid SE → Mediterranean SW → lush forested north; most typical = **semi-arid rolling/flat vast farmland**
Tall square **apartment blocks** everywhere; Muslim-majority — **tall minarets** in most towns; distinctive **brick pavement** in cities
**Area codes**: first digit 2 (west) → 4 (east) (5xx not regional)

**Road numbering**: O-roads (O-xx on markers); D-roads (3-digit number + section number on top — sections increase to the SE); provincial roads: **first 2 digits = province code** (alphabetical 01–76)
**Gen4 truck coverage** → far east + south coast (+pockets west & around Kastamonu)
**North** = lusher & forested; **northeast** = treeless grassland valleys w/ round hills (deep green in Gen3, arid-looking in Gen4)

**Vegetation metas**: Turkish pines + olives → Mediterranean coast; tea → Rize; sunflowers → northwest; vineyards → east of Izmir; cotton → around Şanlıurfa; bananas → around Alanya; greenhouses → south coast west of Adana
**Lake Van**: biggest lake, semi-arid dry grassy hills; dark **volcanic rocks** → Şanlıurfa↔Diyarbakır; barren rocky river valleys → Erzurum↔Artvin + near Hakkâri
**Roof metas**: tiled → western ⅔; flat → southeast + south coast; metal → east; low-pyramid metal → around Lake Van; Nevşehir = big uneven sandstone bricks
**Chevron regions**: yellow-on-black → Balıkesir + Kars; black-on-yellow → north of Izmir; 4-lane divided w/ only double middle lines → eastern half

**Water-tank metas**: vertical tanks = most of Turkey (rare on S/W coast); horizontal-on-vertical → south coast line up to Elâzığ; square w/ angled corners → İskenderun↔Şanlıurfa; white spheres → SE, south of Batman
**European Turkey** (except Istanbul): 6-digit code plate under the yellow electricity warning on poles
Ankara: blue street signs w/ **small round top** (rarely Giresun; newer ones red); Izmir: dark blue, **bulging outward**, usually w/ white Izmir emblem
Red street signs w/ coloured bottom strip: **double-headed arrow with single arrowhead** → almost certainly Istanbul (+ red house numbers)
Trabzon (+Yalvaç): red **oval** street signs & house numbers; Bursa: green signs w/ extruded letters (green fences/guardrails too); Bolu: green but flat, borderless

**Spotlights**: Bodrum = whitewashed flat-roof buildings w/ blue details (Greek look); **Göreme** = sandstone monoliths w/ carved dwellings; **Mount Ararat** (Ağrı Dağı) = tallest peak + smaller Küçük Ağrı SE; Bozcaada island = light-blue/grey tourist signs

**Road spotlights**: E99 S of Şanlıurfa = hazy Gen4 sunrise, flat farmland, N-S angle; D525 S of Söke = dead-straight SSW-NNE winter Gen3 + lots of reed; W shores of Lake Beyşehir & Eğirdir = single-lane road, jagged rock + pines west (Eğirdir's road has a middle line)
